preamble: The shift from the mid-front vowel [e] to a glottal stop [ʔ] is a process of glottal reinforcement. It typically occurs word-initially to provide a 'hard attack' or to separate words in connected speech.

examples.note: Glottal onset: [ʔ] prepended before vowel-initial words. Note: this is ∅→ʔ before /e/, not e→ʔ replacement.
German systematically adds [ʔ] before any initial vowel; this is a phonological rule of onset insertion, not vowel replacement.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2015). A Course in Phonetics (7th ed.). Cengage Learning.
Kohler, K. J. (1977). Einführung in die Phonetik des Deutschen. Erich Schmidt Verlag.
